reviews.text:
I booked this hotel on a whim as there were no jet 2 reviews on site. I did take a look at trip advisor which had a mixed bag and took the gamble anyway, definitely paid off!

The hotel is in a great location, they have a shuttle to the beach which I did not use, as I would say to three of the closest beaches it was less than a 20 walk. The room had a great view of the pool area, spacious enough for two people, smart tv which is a nice touch. Iron is available upon request.

Pros:

- [ ] Great customer service all around, no issues which is hard to come by.
- [ ] Plenty of sunbeds available and no rush to secure one like some other hotels
- [ ] I liked that it was quiet, note this isn’t an adult only hotel, as during my stay there were a family with younger children. Which could be a problem during peak season/school holidays
- [ ] Walkable distance to many good restaurants and room service is adequate too
- [ ] Decor is wonderful and modern
- [ ] Breakfast had a wide selection of fruit, hot food, pastries and alternate milks
- [ ] House keeping staff do a great job of cleaning up the rooms
- [ ] Note the shower gel etc included is currently rituals which is a nice touch and Im glad for once that I didn’t bring my own

Cons (if any)
- [ ] Some construction/building work going on which could be heard during the day, not too loud and was during parable hours where you wouldn’t really be in
- [ ] Minor touches need finishing off
- [ ] If you were here for a long period the breakfast could get a bit repetitive
- [ ] The air con didn’t seem to do the best job of keeping the room cool
- [ ] Could do with music at the main pool during the day

Overall if you’re looking to stay, book it, you won’t regret staying here. One of the best hotels I have stayed at this year

The hotel was amazing! We stayed in the Maretta suite for a week and were very pleased with the room design and amenities. The pools are wonderful, with plenty of sunbeds. The staff is young and friendly, always happy and willing to help. Beach was around 10 minutes away and very accessible. The hotel is in a very quiet area - a less “touristy “ area of Albufeira (no clubs, shops, etc. very nearby).
I must also note we felt very safe and welcomed as a same sex couple :)

The hotel is freshly opened, which can be noticed as there are few little details that could use small improvements (nothing crucial, just little things that would complete the whole experience):
- small hygiene kits in the rooms (containing q-tips, cotton pads maybe mini toothpaste/toothbrush)
-a bit of background music around the pools ( although it is wonderfully quiet, sometimes it gets a little too quiet)
- although the breakfast is quiet diverse it does present the same elements everyday, which can feel a bit repetitive if you have a longer stay.
- the TV is wonderful but unfortunately we didn’t manage to connect our phones to it or use any streaming services
- the toilet is not very sound proof so make sure you’re comfortable with whoever you’re going to stay :)
The best  hotel and staff, beautifull rooms and pool, the view, everything is great, i wish come back already, my family had a wonderfull time, 🥰🥰
I loved the violinist that plays in the hotel ! His music has a modern twist like Bridgerton 🎻🎻
Wonderful place to stay, lovely breakfast and fabulous staff
